ocr_mistral.text:
To the Hon. H. W. Thomas Judge of the Circuit Court of Fauquier County

Humbly complaining your Orators Eli Tackett Washington Baldwin, Willis Jennings & Thomas Dowdy who sue for themselves and the other legatees of John Tarr and respectfully represent that the said John Tarr departed this life on the day of 1859 then being a resident of the County of Fauquier, having first made & published his last will & testament which was admitted to probate & record in the circuit court of Fauquier on the 5th day of April 1859 and on the 21st day of April 1859 the Executrix Elizabeth P. Blackwell having refused to act, Jno P. Phillips and Charles P. Chilton were appointed by said court adms with the will annexed of said accident.

A certified copy of said will is herewith filed marked (A.) and prayed to the letter as a part of this bill.

By this will the testator emancipates all his slaves, and directs that all debts due him be collected that all his personal & real estate shall be sold, and the whole of the proceeds of such collections & sales shall be appropriated for the benefit of his said slaves in the following manner

To the Hon. H. W. Thomas Judge of the
circuit court of Fauquier county

Humbly complaining your
Orators Eli Tackett Washington Bald-
win Willis Jennings & Thomas Dowdy
who sue for themselves and the other
legatees of John Tarr decd respectfully
represent that the said John Tarr
departed this life on the     day of
1859 then being a resident of the County
of Fauquier, having first made &
published his last will & testament
which was admitted to probi &
record in the circuit court of
Fauquier on the 5th day of April 1859
and on the 21st day of April 1859
the Executrix Elizabeth P. Blackwell
having refused to act. Jno P. Phillips
and Charles P. Chilton were appointed
by said court adms with the will
annexed of said decedents.

A certified copy of said will
is herewith filed marked (A.) and
prayed to be taken as a part of this
bill.

By this will the testator emancipates
all his slaves, and directs that all
debts due him be collected that all
his personal & real estate shall be
sold, and the whole of the proceeds
of such collections & sales shall be
appropriated for the benefit of his
said slaves in the following manner
